title: The n=3 view-obstruction coordinate bound: a sorry-free Lean 4 formalization
description: A sorry-free Lean 4 / Mathlib formalization that every sorted primitive integer triple (p, q, r) with maximal-loneliness deficit D(p,q,r) ≥ 3/14 has bounded top coordinate r ≤ 30 — an identification of the finitely many near-minimal-loneliness ('near-equality') triples for n = 3, closing D(p,q,r) ≥ 3/14 ⟺ (p,q,r) ∈ {(1,2,3),(1,2,6),(1,3,4),(1,5,6),(2,3,5)}. This classification was obtained independently by Y-G Chen, and is related to but DISTINCT from Theorem 7.2 of Kravitz, Combinatorial Theory 1 (2021) (which instead characterizes the maximal-loneliness value spectrum: ML = s/(3s+1) or ML ≥ 1/3); attribution corrected per N. Kravitz, private communication, 2026. The unbounded tail (r > 30) is closed by a from-scratch arithmetic-progression covering (not the three-distance theorem — the bounded regime makes the simpler covering sufficient); the finite remainder (r ≤ 30) is closed by a verified enumeration. The strict coordinate bound rests on only Lean's three standard axioms (propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound), confirmed by #print axioms; the full classification capstones additionally use native_decide, disclosed in the source.
